text.title: Requirements Lifecycle Management Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the requirements-lifecycle methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale and is referenced from `content/06-decision-tree.xml`.

rule.statement: Step 1 (define state machine): Document allowed transitions + approvers per transition.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the define state machine gate.

rule.statement: Step 2 (instrument): Add state field + transitions array per record.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the instrument gate.

rule.statement: Step 3 (process change): Run impact analysis → multi-role sign-off → apply transition.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the process change gate.

rule.statement: Step 4 (audit): Audit script verifies forward-only + immutable history.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the audit gate.

rule.statement: If preconditions for requirements-lifecycle do not hold, do not apply this methodology; route to an alternative as the decision tree suggests.
rule.rationale: Methodologies misapplied to wrong contexts create overhead without value; explicit skip preserves the agent's budget.
